evals.prompt: Review this SOW scope section for risk: 'Consulting firm will provide comprehensive digital transformation consulting including strategy development, process redesign, technology implementation, and change management support. All work will be done using commercially reasonable efforts to deliver a successful transformation.'
evals.expected_output: Should identify multiple critical risks: 'comprehensive' and 'all work' are unbounded and undefined, no specific deliverables listed, 'successful transformation' is subjective with no success criteria defined, 'as needed' language creates open-ended scope, 'commercially reasonable efforts' provides no accountability. Should recommend: replace with specific deliverables (strategy document, process maps, RFP template, training plan), quantify scope (e.g., '5 process redesign workshops,' 'up to 10 FTE-days of implementation support'), define success metrics explicitly, replace vague language with measurable commitments.

evals.prompt: Help me identify scope creep risk in this SOW. Fixed fee: $150K. Scope: 'Conduct discovery, design customer journey mapping, create experience recommendations, present findings to leadership team.' Our estimate was 200 FTE-hours at $150/hour = $30K in labor. We also estimated $20K in tools/software. That leaves $100K margin. But we don't have revision limits or presentation approval process defined.
evals.expected_output: Should identify margin risk: $30K labor + $20K tools = $50K cost, leaving $100K margin sounds good but may evaporate if client demands changes. Should flag: no revision round limits for journey map, no definition of what 'recommendations' means or how many, no presentation structure defined (1 presentation or multiple?), no approval/acceptance process. Should recommend adding: 'Up to 2 revision rounds for journey map based on documented feedback,' 'Up to 15 recommendations in priority order,' 'Up to 2 presentation sessions for leadership alignment,' 'Client has 5 business days to provide feedback; deemed accepted if no response.' Should also add: 'Additional revisions beyond 2 rounds, additional presentations, or data deeper than agreed workshop attendance available as change order at $150/hour.'

evals.prompt: Rewrite this client responsibilities section to be more protective: 'Client will provide necessary support and cooperation throughout the engagement.'
evals.expected_output: Should be much more specific and enforceable: 'Client will: (1) Provide stakeholder availability of minimum 10 hours per week for interviews, workshops, and feedback sessions. (2) Designate a single point of contact for approvals and decision-making with authority to approve deliverables. (3) Provide access to existing systems, databases, and documentation within 3 business days of request. (4) Consolidate internal feedback into single written response within 5 business days of each deliverable review. (5) Provide data/content in agreed format; if data is not in specified format, consulting firm's effort to reformat is billable at $150/hour. (6) Timely approvals per schedule in Section X; if timeline windows are exceeded, overall project timeline extends proportionally. Failure to meet these responsibilities may impact delivery timeline and final outcomes.'

evals.prompt: Flag all risky language in this SOW excerpt: 'We commit to ensuring that the system achieves high availability and excellent performance. We will guarantee that all data is secure and fully compliant. The system will support unlimited users and unlimited transactions. We promise to provide ongoing support and continuous optimization as needed.'
evals.expected_output: Should flag each risky phrase: 'ensuring high availability' = no metric (99.9%? 99%?), 'excellent performance' = undefined, 'guarantee...security and fully compliant' = absolute claim with liability exposure, 'unlimited users' and 'unlimited transactions' = no actual bounds, 'ongoing support as needed' = indefinite duration and scope, 'continuous optimization' = undefined effort and timeline. Should recommend: 'System will maintain 99.5% uptime SLA per Section X. Performance targets: [specific metrics]. Security: will implement [specific controls per standard: SOC2, ISO27001]. Data compliance: [specific regulations], subject to audit per Section X. System supports [specific user and transaction volume per testing results]. Post-launch support: 30 days at 40 hours/month for issue triage and workarounds. Optimization: identified improvements documented in quarterly review; additional optimization available via change order.'

evals.prompt: Create an out-of-scope section for this fixed-fee digital transformation SOW. The scope includes: discovery, design, training materials, post-launch support for 30 days. What should be explicitly excluded?
evals.expected_output: Should create an out-of-scope section that explicitly excludes: 'The following are NOT included in this SOW unless modified by written change order: (1) Data migration, historical data cleanup, or data transformation. (2) Custom code development or implementation beyond training/setup guidance. (3) Third-party system integration or API development. (4) Regulatory compliance review or legal analysis. (5) Ongoing support beyond 30-day hypercare period. (6) Stakeholder training delivery beyond recorded training materials (live training is change order). (7) Change management beyond strategy and training materials (client responsibility). (8) Performance optimization or tuning. (9) Custom reporting dashboard development. (10) Photography, videography, or professional illustration (uses UI screenshots only).'

evals.prompt: Draft a change order clause for this SOW that protects margin and provides clear process: Fixed fee $200K, duration 4 months, scope per Section 2.
evals.expected_output: Should draft: 'CHANGE ORDER PROCESS: (1) Client requests for work outside defined scope must be submitted in writing to [PM name/title]. (2) Consulting firm will assess request and provide written estimate within 3 business days, including: scope description, estimated hours, fee, and timeline impact. (3) Client must approve change order in writing before work begins. (4) Change orders are billed separately from base SOW at rate of $150/hour (or per deliverable as negotiated). (5) Change order timeline is in addition to base project timeline; work will not begin until written approval received. (6) All change orders must be signed by [authority] before execution. (7) If multiple change requests accumulate beyond [X hours/amount], consulting firm may propose scope deferral or extended timeline.'

evals.prompt: Stress-test this SOW for margin risk: Fixed fee $300K, duration 6 months, team: 1 senior consultant (80% allocation), 1 mid-level consultant (100% allocation), 1 junior consultant (50% allocation). Cost of team: estimated at $180K for 6 months. Budget for tools, travel, admin: $20K. Scope: discovery, design, implementation support, training. Red flag anything that could erode margin.
evals.expected_output: Should identify several margin risks: (1) Cost is $200K ($180K + $20K) leaving $100K margin, but this assumes perfect project execution with no scope expansion or timeline slip. (2) 'Implementation support' is vague - could consume unlimited effort if not bounded. (3) No revision limits - if client demands 5+ design revision cycles, team burns budget fast. (4) 'Training' scope is undefined - is this delivery or materials-only? (5) 6-month timeline with fixed team allocation assumes no delays (client delays, blocked dependencies could compress schedule and require overtime). Should recommend: Define implementation support hours (e.g., 'up to 100 hours'), cap design revisions at 2-3 rounds, clarify training as materials-only unless otherwise specified, build change order process to capture out-of-scope requests, establish contingency for timeline slippage.

evals.prompt: This SOW says 'deliverables must be approved by client before project can proceed to next phase.' But there's no acceptance criteria or timeline. What's the risk, and how should we fix it?
evals.expected_output: Should identify risk: Without acceptance criteria, client can reject deliverables as not meeting 'quality' standards (subjective). Without timeline, client can delay approval indefinitely, blocking project progress and team allocation. Should fix with: 'DELIVERABLE ACCEPTANCE: (1) Acceptance criteria for each deliverable defined in Section X. (2) Client review window: 5 business days from delivery date. (3) Client must provide consolidated written feedback. (4) If no feedback received within 5 business days, deliverable deemed accepted. (5) Acceptance revisions: up to 2 rounds of revisions included per deliverable, based on documented feedback. (6) Post-acceptance changes: if revisions are requested after acceptance, they are treated as change orders. (7) If client-requested revisions exceed 2 rounds, additional revisions are billable at $150/hour. (8) Project timeline extends proportionally if client exceeds approval windows.'
